title: Charmed Life - Chapter 18 (Fragment 6)
text:
There was a scream of triumph from under the nodding hats of the audienceвАФthe scream of true witchcraft, which made the sunlight tremble. Chrestomanci, even more disheveled than the Willing Warlock, was dragged out from between the pillars. His tall gray hat rolled near CatвАЩs feet and Henry Nostrum stamped on it, with the greatest satisfaction. Cat tried to get out from under Henry NostrumвАЩs hand while he did it. And he found he could not move. Mr. Nostrum had seen to that with Mrs. SharpвАЩs postcard. Cat had to face the fact that he was as helpless as Chrestomanci seemed to be.

вАЬSo it is true!вАЭ Henry Nostrum said joyously, as the Willing Warlock bundled Chrestomanci towards the apple tree. вАЬThe touch of silver conquers ChrestomanciвАФthe great Chrestomanci!вАЭ

вАЬYes. IsnвАЩt it a nuisance?вАЭ Chrestomanci remarked. He was dragged to the apple tree and pushed against it. William Nostrum hurried over to his brother and pulled the watch-chain off HenryвАЩs bulging waistcoat. Two silver watch-chains from two such ample brothers were more than enough to tie Chrestomanci to the tree. William Nostrum hastily twisted the ends into two charmed knots and stood back rubbing his hands. The audience screamed eldritch laughter and clapped. Chrestomanci sagged as if he were tired. His hair hung over his face, his tie was under his left ear, and there was green from the bark of the tree all over his dove-gray coat. Cat felt somehow ashamed to look at him in that state. But Chrestomanci seemed quite composed. вАЬNow youвАЩve got me all tied up in silver, what do you propose doing?вАЭ he said.

William NostrumвАЩs eyes swirled joyfully about. вАЬOh, the worst we can, my dear sir,вАЭ he said. вАЬBe assured of that. WeвАЩre sick of you imposing restraints on us, you see. Why shouldnвАЩt we go out and conquer other worlds? Why shouldnвАЩt we use dragonsвАЩ blood? Why shouldnвАЩt we be as wicked as we want? Answer me that, sir!вАЭ

вАЬYou might find the answer for yourself, if you thought,вАЭ Chrestomanci suggested. But his voice was drowned in the yelling from the assembled witches and necromancers. While they shouted, Janet began edging quietly towards the tree. She supposed Cat dared not move with Henry NostrumвАЩs hand on his shoulder, and she felt someone ought to do something.

вАЬOh, yes,вАЭ said Henry Nostrum, cock-a-hoop with pleasure. вАЬWe are taking the arts of magic into our own hands today. This world will be ours by this evening. Come Halloween, dear sir, we shall be going out to conquer every other world we know. We are going to destroy you, my dear fellow, and your power. But before we do that, of course, we shall have to destroy this garden.вАЭ

Chrestomanci looked thoughtfully down at his hands, hanging limply in the silver handcuffs. вАЬI shouldnвАЩt advise that,вАЭ he said. вАЬThis garden has things in it from the dawn of all the worlds. ItвАЩs a good deal stronger than I am. YouвАЩd be striking at the roots of witchcraftвАФand youвАЩd find it shockingly hard to destroy.вАЭ

вАЬAh,вАЭ said Henry Nostrum. вАЬBut we know we canвАЩt destroy you unless we destroy the garden, my wily sir. And donвАЩt think we donвАЩt know how to destroy the garden.вАЭ He lifted his free hand and clapped Cat on the other shoulder with it. вАЬThe means are here.вАЭ

Janet, at that moment, stumbled over the block of stone that lay in the grass near the apple tree. вАЬDratitude!вАЭ she said and fell heavily across it. The audience pointed and screamed with laughter, which annoyed her very much. She glared around the circle of Sunday bonnets and hats.

vocabulary.definition: Enough or more than enough; plentiful.
vocabulary.example: Two silver watch-chains from two such ample brothers were more than enough to tie Chrestomanci to the tree.

vocabulary.definition: Skills or practices related to using magic.
vocabulary.example: We are taking the arts of magic into our own hands today.

vocabulary.definition: Gathered together in one place for a common purpose.
vocabulary.example: But his voice was drowned in the yelling from the assembled witches and necromancers.

vocabulary.definition: Swelling outwards; protruding.
vocabulary.example: pulled the watch-chain off HenryвАЩs bulging waistcoat.

vocabulary.definition: Knots believed to have magical properties or spells placed on them.
vocabulary.example: William Nostrum hastily twisted the ends into two charmed knots and stood back rubbing his hands.

vocabulary.definition: Very pleased and excited; triumphant or boastful.
vocabulary.example: Oh, yes,вАЭ said Henry Nostrum, cock-a-hoop with pleasure.

vocabulary.definition: Having control over feelings; calm and self-possessed.
vocabulary.example: But Chrestomanci seemed quite composed.

vocabulary.definition: To gain control of something through force or effort.
vocabulary.example: Why shouldnвАЩt we go out and conquer other worlds?

vocabulary.definition: Untidy, messy, or in disorder.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci, even more disheveled than the Willing Warlock, was dragged out from between the pillars.

vocabulary.definition: A made-up or humorous exclamation to express mild annoyance or frustration.
vocabulary.example: Janet, at that moment, stumbled over the block of stone ... вАЬDratitude!вАЭ she said.

vocabulary.definition: To be overwhelmed by noise or sound to the point of being inaudible.
vocabulary.example: But his voice was drowned in the yelling from the assembled witches and necromancers.

vocabulary.definition: Uncanny, eerie or supernatural laughter.
vocabulary.example: The audience screamed eldritch laughter and clapped.

vocabulary.definition: To stare angrily or fiercely.
vocabulary.example: She glared around the circle of Sunday bonnets and hats.

vocabulary.definition: A type of aircraft which can hover and fly backwards or sideways.
vocabulary.example: Not applicable in this text fragment.

vocabulary.definition: To enforce limitations or restrictions on someone or something.
vocabulary.example: WeвАЩre sick of you imposing restraints on us.

vocabulary.definition: People who practice necromancy, a form of magic involving communication with the dead.
vocabulary.example: But his voice was drowned in the yelling from the assembled witches and necromancers.

vocabulary.definition: A person, thing, or circumstance causing inconvenience or annoyance.
vocabulary.example: вАЬYes. IsnвАЩt it a nuisance?вАЭ Chrestomanci remarked.

vocabulary.definition: A card for sending messages by mail without an envelope, often with a picture on one side.
vocabulary.example: Mr. Nostrum had seen to that with Mrs. SharpвАЩs postcard.

vocabulary.definition: Measures or conditions that limit or restrict action or behaviour.
vocabulary.example: WeвАЩre sick of you imposing restraints on us.

vocabulary.definition: Dropped or sank down; became limp or weak.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci sagged as if he were tired.

vocabulary.definition: Moved backwards to give space or a better view.
vocabulary.example: William Nostrum hastily twisted the ends into two charmed knots and stood back rubbing his hands.

vocabulary.definition: Attracting attention by being unusual or prominent.
vocabulary.example: YouвАЩd be striking at the roots of witchcraftвАФand youвАЩd find it shockingly hard to destroy.

vocabulary.definition: A great victory or achievement.
vocabulary.example: There was a scream of triumph from under the nodding hats of the audience.

vocabulary.definition: Evil or morally wrong.
vocabulary.example: Why shouldnвАЩt we be as wicked as we want?

vocabulary.definition: Skilful at gaining an advantage, especially deceitfully.
vocabulary.example: And donвАЩt think we donвАЩt know how to destroy the garden, my wily sir.

vocabulary.definition: The practice of magical skills and abilities, especially evil magic.
vocabulary.example: The scream of true witchcraft, which made the sunlight tremble.
